mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns exceed 55% when FEDFUNDS is above 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending expectations, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5% for three consecutive months, invalidating the labor deterioration channel.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both grow greater than 2% month-over-month, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that offsets rate hike headwinds, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns as markets price in soft landing scenarios.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to correlate with positive 5-day returns when FEDFUNDS increases by greater than 0.25% in the prior quarter, with Rank IC consistently negative.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic expansion that supports corporate earnings trajectories, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero predictive power for 60-day forward returns across all historical regimes, with Rank IC consistently near zero over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above 0.4% monthly forces markets to anticipate aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive in greater than 65% of months where CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.4%, invalidating the inflation-fear transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payrolls growth combined with stable or declining federal funds rates signals accommodative monetary policy supporting a robust labor market, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal loses predictive accuracy when PAYEMS growth exceeds 200k but FEDFUNDS remains flat, with forward return direction matching baseline 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where retail sales decline while industrial production rises indicates inventory buildup and weakening consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as earnings revisions turn downward.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative returns when RSAFS falls greater than 1% and INDPRO rises greater than 1%, with positive forward returns occurring in greater than 60% of cases.
